scenes.narration: What if you could build a state of the art translation model with no recurrence and no convolutions at all? In twenty seventeen, eight researchers at Google proposed exactly that.
Before this, the best sequence models read a sentence one word at a time. That made them slow to train, because each step has to wait for the one before it.

props.text: Recurrent models process a sentence one step at a time — inherently sequential.

scenes.narration: The Transformer throws recurrence away entirely, and relies on a single mechanism, attention, so every word can look directly at every other word, all at once, in parallel.

props.text: Dispense with recurrence and convolution. Use attention alone.

scenes.narration: Here is the full architecture. On the left an encoder reads the input sentence; on the right a decoder generates the output. Both are built by stacking the same simple block six times.

props.label: Figure 1
props.caption: The Transformer — an encoder (left) and decoder (right), each a stack of six identical layers.

scenes.narration: Each layer has two sub-layers: multi-head self-attention, then a small position-wise feed-forward network. Both are wrapped in a residual connection and layer normalization.

props.heading: Inside one layer
items: Multi-head self-attention, then a position-wise feed-forward network
Each sub-layer wrapped in a residual connection + layer normalization
Every sub-layer outputs vectors of dimension d_model = 512

scenes.narration: So what is attention? Each word forms three vectors: a query, a key, and a value. The query is compared against every key to decide how much to listen to each value.

props.text: A query asks a question; it is matched against every key to weight the values.

scenes.narration: Concretely, take the dot product of the query with every key, scale it down by the square root of the key dimension d sub k, apply a softmax, and use the result to average the values.

props.heading: Scaled Dot-Product Attention
props.caption: dot product  →  scale by √d_k  →  softmax  →  weighted sum of values

scenes.narration: As a diagram, that is a matmul of queries and keys, a scaling, an optional mask, a softmax, and a final matmul with the values.
Rather than doing this once, the Transformer runs attention eight times in parallel. Each head uses its own learned projections, and the heads are concatenated and projected back down.

props.label: Figure 2
props.caption: Multi-head attention: h = 8 parallel heads, concatenated then projected.

scenes.narration: With no recurrence, the model has no built-in sense of word order. So the authors add positional encodings, fixed sine and cosine waves, directly to the input embeddings.

props.heading: Positional Encoding
props.caption: fixed sinusoids of varying frequency, added to the input embeddings

scenes.narration: Why bother? Self-attention connects any two positions in a single step, shortening the path that gradients travel, and unlike recurrence it parallelizes across the whole sequence.
The payoff was dramatic: twenty eight point four BLEU on English to German, a new state of the art forty one point eight on English to French, trained in just three and a half days on eight GPUs.
By showing that attention alone was enough, this paper became the foundation for nearly every large language model that followed.
